feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: It's a wrap!
entry.content: Congratulations on all who completed the March Developer Challenge! Well done 👏🎉

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
@Pratham47 it is great to meet you in the community. My name is Stephanie and I am based in the US. I work in SAP Community on programs and with SAP Influencers Directory | SAP Community.

May I ask your areas of interest and where you have or plan to obtain product expertise?

Be sure to JOIN interest groups to stay engaged. Interest Groups | SAP Community Best of luck.

entry.title: Re: Applying the pipeline concept for a point-to-point integration scenario
entry.content:
Hi,

When we tried to build Receiver Determination flow, there is a Header "SAP_ReceiverIndex" declared in "Get receiver name" Content Modifier, but when we see the XSLT mapping , didn't find that "Index" field in it. Do we have any specific reason why we are using the Index or the Index field is missing in XSLT mapping.

Kindly need your help to resolve this issue.

entry.title: Knowledge Graphs on Datasphere and HANA Cloud. The differences
entry.content:
The ability to enhance RAG with Vector search, Graph knowledge bases, or SQL Queries is fundamental. SAP recently announced the Datasphere capability to create Graphs, and there are some key differences between the graph capabilities in SAP HANA Cloud, the new announcement SAP Datasphere knowledge graph and the Intergation Suite capability called Graph:

1. SAP HANA Cloud has had a powerful graph engine for analyzing complex relationships in business data for a while now. It allows creating graph workspaces from existing tables/views, querying graphs using openCypher, writing custom graph algorithms, and visualizing graphs.

 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-hana-graph-resources/ba-p/13505490

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oaSQu9laYKE

2. Datasphere Creating knowledge graphs manually is very costly, so SAP Datasphere comes to help by automatically pre-populating them using content already available in SAP Datasphere like connections to source systems, tables, foreign keys etc. SAP Datasphere knowledge graph is a new capability announced in March 2024. As data is onboarded and integrated into SAP Datasphere, it automatically creates an ontology representing the relationships in the data, including business context from SAP sources like S/4HANA

caption by SAP. Currently in Preview

 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PQrFjthwOWQ&t=169

https://news.sap.com/2024/03/future-of-data-driven-business-new-sap-datasphere-capabilities/

3. SAP Graph as an Integration Suite capability 

Parallel to building Graphs from Databases, SAP Graph is an alternative unified API available on the SAP Business Technology Platform that provides access to a connected graph of SAP-managed business data, regardless of where the data resides.

SAP Graph is intended for building extension applications, such as workflow applications using low-code tools or event-driven applications, and it supports modern open standards like OData v4 and GraphQL

 

 

https://podcast.opensap.info/cloud-platform/2022/04/28/episode-82-sap-graph-the-easy-to-use-api-of-the-intelligent-enterprise/

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/graph-new-in-sap-integration-suite/ba-p/13555887

Conclusion

While the SAP HANA Cloud graph is more for custom graph algorithm development and analysis, the SAP Datasphere knowledge graph is an automated capability that provides a rich context for generative AI use cases by capturing complex data relationships and metadata. The two serve different but complementary purposes in the SAP data and analytics portfolio.

Additional information:
https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/how-use-sap-hana-cloud-graph-model-analyze-complex-your-holitschke

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-hana-graph-resources/ba-p/13505490

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/native-hana-vs-sap-datasphere-findings/qaq-p/12689997

https://www.techzine.eu/blogs/data-management/117331/sap-expands-datasphere-with-knowledge-graph-and...

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sap-datasphere-is-an-alternative-of-sap-hana-modelling-w...

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/access-sap-hana-cloud-underneath-of-sap-dat...

https://developers.sap.com/group.hana-aa-graph-overview.html

entry.title: Re: Use SAP Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (CALM) System to Monitor Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3
entry.content: Thank you for sharing a beautiful in-sight on this topic. Truly helpful!!

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration – Connecting to Messaging Systems using the AMQP Adapter
entry.content:
If you don't see a difference, probably the amount of messages in the queues is not large enough? Is there a real backlog in the queue that needs to be handled? If so and there is no upscaling of the processes even if configured I would suggest you open a ticket on LOD-HCI-PI-CON-SOAP

Thank you

Mandy

entry.title: Re: Using Custom MDF to Record Education Information and Identify the Highest Education Level
entry.content: Thanks for sharing this valuable content! From an HR perspective, exploring tools such as NCEDCloud can further improve educational processes by enabling effective interventions and improved learning outcomes.

@Ninhs24 - I'll reach out via email

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
@jenifer_ranjani 
From what I can see, the account with which you posted this reply is actually your older account, from 2022.

Where exactly are you trying to create the blog post? Regardless of rank, you should be able to submit a blog post in a group. And since you have Participant rank (see more about ranks here https://community.sap.com/t5/what-s-new/let-the-gamification-begin/ba-p/311892), you also have authorization to submit a blog post in the Product & Technology categories.

I would try logging out and logging in again, then see if you still get the error when you try to post. If you do, please write an email to community@sap.com and the team will investigate the issue.

Regards,
Audrey

entry.title: Re: Stay Secure with SAP Malware Scanning on the SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:

Thanks for wonderful blog, I have a query on the cost side of using this service. As on the discovery portal it says as Always Free but are there any charges per call we make to this API?

Many Thanks,
Harsh

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP: Import Purchasing Data from Excel Files and Load into Order Based Planning using Python
entry.content:
Could you please share details of the dataset j5 used in this commit statement?

if x.status_code == 201:
#Commit the POST Request
    y = requests.post(f"{DATA_URL}/SAP__self.Commit",data=j5,auth=(USERNAME, PASSWORD), verify=False, headers = headers, cookies = c )
​

 We are getting the error of incompatible data type.

Thanks,

Anishka Moona

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Gerhard,

Currently as I understand based on the Incoterms stage building 2 stages are created in the FU e.g. Delivering plant to Port of loading and Port of Loading to receiving plant (depending on the Incoterms).

But if the requirement here is to split the FU into 3 stages i.e 1. Delivering plant to Port of Loading 2. Port of loading to Port of Discharge 3. Port of Discharge to Receiving plant to capture since we can have different carriers managing Pre-carriage leg, Main-Carriage leg, and On-Carriage leg. Is there a way to manage such scenario in Advanced Intercompany STO process? Or is it going to be a manual activity? 

Thanks

BR,

Jenifer

Thank you for this wonderful opportunity.

 

I’m interested in this great opportunity! I’m 100% in, even if I don’t know if I have a chance to be chosen. I worked with some accounting systems but SAP is the best one. I’d like to become the SAP consultant in future, I’m a woman and I’d like to be a part of professional SAP world. I’m also exited, going to find a proper training path for me! Stay tuned! 

Regard,

Nguyen Mai Nhu Luan

entry.title: Enhancing Performance in SAP Web Applications: Strategies and Best Practices
entry.content:
Understanding the Performance Landscape:

Performance in SAP web applications is a multifaceted issue, encompassing aspects such as response time, data processing efficiency, and user interface fluidity. The complexity of SAP systems, coupled with the demand for high-speed data processing and real-time analytics, makes performance optimization a critical task for developers and administrators.

Key Strategies for Optimization:

  • Efficient Use of SAP Fiori and UI5: SAP Fiori and SAP UI5 are at the forefront of SAP’s user experience. To optimize performance, it’s essential to minimize the number of round-trips between the client and server, use asynchronous calls judiciously, and leverage caching mechanisms effectively.
  • Optimizing SAP HANA Usage: SAP HANA's in-memory computing capabilities offer significant performance benefits. To fully leverage these, developers should optimize data models, use proper indexing, and ensure efficient query design to minimize data processing times.
  • Code Optimization: In any SAP web application, clean and efficient coding practices are paramount. This includes minimizing unnecessary database calls, optimizing loops and internal tables, and adhering to SAP’s best coding practices.
  • Load Balancing and Scalability: Implementing load balancing techniques and ensuring the application is scalable to handle increased load are vital. This involves balancing the load across servers and optimizing resource allocation.
  • Effective Use of Caching: Caching frequently accessed data reduces the number of database hits, significantly improving response times. Appropriate use of browser and server-side caching can lead to substantial performance gains.
  • Regular Performance Testing: Conducting regular performance testing, including stress and load testing, helps in identifying bottlenecks and areas for improvement. Tools like SAP Load Runner can be instrumental in this process.

Best Practices for SAP Web Application Performance:

  • Monitor Regularly: Continuous monitoring of application performance is essential. Tools like SAP Solution Manager and SAP Fiori Monitor can provide valuable insights into application performance metrics.
  • Optimize Network Performance: Ensure that the network configuration is optimized for the best performance. This includes looking at aspects like bandwidth, latency, and data packet sizes.
  • Responsive Design Considerations: For mobile or responsive web applications, performance optimization also includes UI elements that are lightweight and adaptable to different screen sizes without compromising on performance.
  • User Experience Focus: A performant application is not just about speed. It’s also about how quickly and efficiently users can complete their tasks. This means optimizing navigation flows, search functionalities, and overall application layout.
  • Cloud Integration: When using SAP Cloud Platform, leverage cloud capabilities for better performance. This includes utilizing cloud-based processing power and storage effectively.

Performance optimization in SAP web applications is a continuous process, not a one-time fix. As technology evolves and business needs change, strategies for optimization must also adapt. By focusing on efficient coding practices, leveraging SAP's advanced tools, and maintaining a user-centric approach, organizations can significantly enhance the performance and user experience of their SAP web applications.

entry.title: Re: MRP: Schedule Lines
entry.content:
Hi,

so, if you set in MRP indicator (1) you will... become a PReq??

Thanks in advance

Jorge Santos

entry.title: Re: Unleashing AI and Machine Learning in Sales: Advanced Price-Volume Forecasting with SAP Analytic
entry.content:
Hi Dirk,

Great post to illustrate how a predictive forecast can incorporate price as an influencer!

My next questions would now be:
1) Regarding 'optimized price planning': Based on the planned volumes, what is the optimal price to reach highest revenue (optimization problem)?
2) Regarding 'reducing manual effort': Currently SAC planners need to copy the price into each forecast period granularity, and into the same model. Can this activity be avoided? (e.g. by using an easy valid from/to date like in ECC)

entry.title: Re: Additional SAP Community feeds 📡
entry.content:
Hi @ajmaradiaga 

Is there any chance this functionality somehow got broken? During the last days, I noticed that my feed reader (Feedly) suddenly started to show a ton of entries per tag:

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Creating Pay Component in One Time Portlet using Integration Center
entry.content:
Hi lsharma

Thanks for your post

we have the following requirement:

Automatic creation of the job info and compensation portlet in Success Factors

The compensation portlet must be updated with the next pay scale level

The new salary structure (pay scale type, pay scale area, pay scale group and pay scale level) must be updated in the job information portlet

These are the conditions:

The employee must have been working in the company for 3 months.

The employee must have completed the mandatory courses for newly hired employees, This information is in a custom portlet.

We need to create an integration that runs every Friday to update the job information and compensation portlet for eligible employees.

The company has around 25000 employees

Can we solve the requiriment with integration center?

Thanks in advance 

 

Regards

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud – SAP S/4HANA extensibility – May 2023 update
entry.content:
Hi Boris,

If you could provide us with your great support with those questions.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-deploy-sapui5-apps-on-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/qaq-p/13654859#M4823723

Regards,
Anthony Ramos
